en: Have you noticed?
Women went from girls to women, men went from men to boys.
ko: 여자들은 소녀에서 여성으로, 남자들은 남자에서 소년으로 변했어.

en: This is a problem in the big picture, do you know what I mean?
ko: 이건 큰 그림에서 문제야, 무슨 말인지 알겠어?

en: Yeah.
I'm gonna have another.
Another? You sure about that? Yeah.
Okay, here's my theory about this.
We all grew up during the take your daughter to work day thing, right?
ko: 우리는 모두 '딸을 직장에 데려가자' 행사 때 자랐잖아?

en: So, we were always told we could be anything, do anything.
ko: 그래서 우리는 항상 무엇이든 될 수 있다고 들었어.

en: And I think guys got, maybe not left behind, but not quite as nurtured, you know?
ko: 남자들은 아마 뒤처진 건 아니지만, 그렇게 잘 보살핌을 받지 못한 것 같아.

en: I mean, like, we were the generation of, you go girl, we had Oprah.
And I wonder sometimes how guys fit in, you know?
They still seem to be trying to figure it out.
ko: 그들은 여전히 그걸 알아내려고 애쓰고 있어.

en: They're still dressing like little boys, they're still playing video games.
ko: 여전히 어린 소년처럼 옷을 입고, 비디오 게임을 하고 있어.

en: Well, they've gotten great.
How in one generation have men gone from guys like Jack Nicholson and Harrison Ford to...
ko: 어떻게 한 세대 만에 남자들이 잭 니콜슨과 해리슨 포드 같은 사람들에서...

en: Hmm? Hmm.
Take them! Here.
Die and grieve, you know?
Look and learn, boys, because if you ask me, this is what we're gonna do.
ko: 보고 배우렴, 남자들, 왜냐하면 내 생각에 우리는 이렇게 할 거니까.
